rationale: The LLM at this capability level (basic scripting, common vulnerability pattern recognition, template-based exploit generation) provides some marginal assistance at the Execution step by generating PowerShell/scripting payloads and suggesting LOLBins techniques, but OC3 actors already possess these capabilities at a high baseline (90%). The primary failure mode at this step - EDR detection of execution attempts - requires more sophisticated evasion than a low-capability LLM can provide. The estimated uplift is approximately 2-3 percentage points, bringing the median to 92%, reflecting that the LLM serves as a modest efficiency aid rather than a meaningful capability multiplier at this already-high baseline step.
